query: For what reasons can I get a leave of absence, and what does it cost me?

queries: For what reasons can I get a leave of absence, and what does it cost me?

The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text: apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
retrieved.text: Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the relevant authority whether you can receive family or student financial aid. - Insurance: Co-insurance with relatives or student self-insurance may not be possible. Please enquire directly with your insurance company.

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: Leave of absence during the semester
retrieved.text: A leave of absence during the semester is only possible if you can prove that one of the following reasons occurred and only became relevant after the start of the semester: / - Pregnancy Proof: - parent-child pass with calculated date of birth or medical confirmation and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. - Childcare Proof: - confirmation of registration ("Meldebestätigung") of the applicant and - confirmation of registration ("Meldebestätigung") of the child and - birth certificate of the child and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. Please note: - If you raise your own children, a leave of absence is possible until the children reach the age of legal majority. Both parents can be on leave at the same time. - Care for relatives Proof: - proof of kinship (birth/marriage certificate) and - notification of care level or medical confirmation regarding the need for care (no diagnosis required) and - statutory declaration from the applicant stating that the relative is being cared for by the applicant and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. - Medical condition that verifiably prevents progress in the degree programme Proof: - [medical confirmation (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Beurlaubung/Formular_medizinische_Bestaetigung_Beurlaubung_EN.pdf) confirming that you have been or will

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: Leave of absence before the start of the semester
retrieved.text: older than 12 weeks. - As proof of self-employment, a corresponding confirmation (tax book extract, cash register contract, etc.) in conjunction with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment can be accepted. - Settlement of issues with authorities abroad This concerns a significant interference with your studies for at least four weeks due to the settlement of issues with authorities abroad. Proof: - summons, official order or other confirmation letter by an authority - Current situation in Ukraine Due to the current situation in Ukraine, students from Ukraine and the Russian Federation can apply for leave of absence in summer semester 2026 until 28 February. Write a short explanation for your prevention from studying in the online form in the Servicedesk.

heading_path: Leave of absence before the start of the semester
retrieved.text: evident from them. - Military service, training service or civilian service Proof: - confirmation by the military or civil service agency - Voluntary year of environmental protection, memorial, peace or social service abroad Proof: - confirmation by the agency or mediating authority - Temporary impairment in relation to a disability Proof: - disability card and - medical or comparable confirmation - Professional activities This refers to at least four weeks of significant impairment of the pursuit of the degree programme due to professional activity or further training and qualification measures due to professional activity. Proof: - [confirmation of the employer (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Beurlaubung/Formular_Dienststelle_EN.pdf) of a significant interference with your studies during at least four weeks due to your employment which exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügigkeitsgrenze") - in case of training and qualification measures: additionally, a confirmation of participation/registration by the training institute including number of lessons and course duration Please note: - Educational measures that are not related to a professional activity are not considered as reasons for a leave of absence. - Pay slips cannot be accepted as proof. Employment contracts are accepted in exceptional cases, provided they are not older than 12 weeks. - As proof of self-employment, a corresponding confirmation (tax book extract, cash register contract, etc.) in conjunction with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment can be accepted. - Settlement of issues with authorities

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: Reimbursement of the tuition fee Already paid tuition fee can be reimbursed for certain, precisley defined reasons. This is the case, for example, if your application for remission or leave of absence has been granted. Further examples: You have completed your studies before the end of the admission period or you have paid too much. Related Links: - [Reimbursement of tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) Related entries: - [Leave of absence](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30834&cHash=6c0061df445fb72ace014b66819ed159) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Remission of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31930&cHash=ec520748e744f3ee49dc41584e03d431) - Remission of the tuition fee Citizens of an EU/EEA country or equivalent non-EU/EEA citizens can apply for remission of the tuition fee provided that: - it has been submitted on time and in full and - there is a recognised reason for remission. The application for remission is granted, but you have already paid the tuition fee? Read the information on tuition fee reimbursement and note the application deadlines. Related Links: - [Remission](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) - [Reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Equal status with EU/EEA citizens with regard to the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31127&cHash=a9fb293a7c3370b27dd98f693d1ca082) - [Reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31959&cHash=7d2042e16b5c6049dc06cc8398971bfa) - Residence permit ("visa") Residence permits authorise persons from third countries to settle in Austria (for a limited period) and/or to work here. Please enquire about this topic at an Austrian representation authority (embassy, consulate) or the Municipal Department 35 (MA 35). A residence permit can also have an influence on the amount of

Nr. 183/1947 idgF - Research grant holders at the University of Vienna Proof: award letter - ÖH-Funktion If you perform a function in the Austrian Students' Union, you can apply for remission of the tution fee. Only use the [provided from for remission for students working in the Students' Union (PDF, only available in German)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Formular_Erlass_OEH_Funktion.pdf). You can only apply for remission for four semesters. - Employees If you work and study at the University of Vienna at the same time, the tuition fee will be automatically remitted (regardless of citizenship). Prerequisites - You were employed at the University of Vienna for at least 90 days during the previous semester. - You are not studying at another Austrian University. Please note, that the full fee will be prescribed at the beginning of the payment period. This changes automatically after a few days. Only pay the Students' Union fee then. [You can find further information in the Intranet of the University of Vienna (login required).](https://wiki.univie.ac.at/pages/viewpage.action?pageId=400589235) - Students with Ukrainian citizenship The tuition fee for winter semester 2026/27 is

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
retrieved.text: the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - Application for leave of absence granted After paying the tuition fee your [application for leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) was granted? A reimbursement is only possible if you have submitted your application for leave of absence before 30 November (for the winter semester) and 30 April (for the summer semester). - Application for remission granted Your application for remission of the tuition fee was granted after paying the tuition fee? The tuition fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too high You have paid a too high fee? The difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too little You have paid too little and are therefore no longer admitted at the University of Vienna? The fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - Reimbursement of the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is

retrieved.title: ABC of terminology
retrieved.text: of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=42309&cHash=54e30ba32dac123141345da256f85101) - Educational institution ID The educational institution ID is a unique label of a higher education institution and precedes the degree programme code. Both can be found in the Study overview on u:space and on the student record sheet. Related Links: - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast) Related entries: - [u:space](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31311&cHash=571431f2f0b27f0eec7e3fbf0fcdd580) - Educational leave An educational leave is a time off from work to complete education and continue education and training. During an educational leave, the Austrian Public Employment Service (AMS) pays a continuing education allowance under certain prerequisites. Please obtain information directly from the AMS. As part of educational leave, you can study in a non-degree programme. Please consider the consequences of a non-degree programme if you have a secondary-school leaving certificate. Related Links: - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/) - [Austrian Public Employment Service](https://www.ams.at/arbeitsuchende/topicliste/bildungskarenz-oesterreich) (in German) Related entries: - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - Eligible degree programme The admission to a master programme is only possible if you have previously completed an eligible degree programme. After having applied for admission to the degree programme, the Admission Office will evaluate the eligibility of your previous degree. If it is found to be eligible, but some exams (of max. 30 ECTS) are missing, supplementray exams are prescribed in your admission letter. The Master Access Guide illustrates which degree programmes enable you to continue with a certain master programme. Related Links: - Master Access Guide Related

retrieved.title: Leave of absence
retrieved.text:
You have the option of [closing your studies](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) and resuming them at a later date. Consider this option especially if it is likely that your studies will be interrupted for a long time. Please inform yourself in advance about possible conditions for [resuming your studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/). For master programmes with an admission procedure and the bachelor programme in Psychology, you must successfully complete the entrance exam/selection procedure again. For the bachelor programme in Sport Science and the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education, you may have to take the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) again. Please contact the [SSC Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/studienservicecenter-und-studienprogrammleitung/) for more information.

As an alternative to a leave of absence, you may apply for a [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). Please note the respective application deadlines and reasons for remission.

- [§ 21 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), § 67](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

retrieved.title: Remission and reimbursement
retrieved.text:
/

- Remission due to illness: What qualifies als illness?

  Reasons for illness may be, among other things, major operations, chronic illness or also mental impairments. You need a [confirmation by a medical specialist](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Formular_Fachaerztliche_Bestaetigung_Erlass_EN.pdf), that you are thereby impaired from your studies for at least two months during the semester. The diagnosis does not need to be stated.
- What shall I do if I do not have all proofs for applying for remission until the application deadline?

  In this case you must pay the tuition fee. Afterwards, you will be able to apply for remission and reimbursement at the same time. These two applications can be submitted for the winter semester until the following 28/29 February and for the summer semester until 30 September.
- My application for remission of the tuition fee was granted, but I have already paid the tuition fee. What can I do?

  If your application for remission was granted, you can apply for the reimbursement of the tuition fee in order to get the money transferred back to your bank account. The application for reimbursement can also be submitted at the same time as the application for remission of the tuition fee.
- What happens after I have applied for remission/reimbursement?

  The processing of your application takes approximately four weeks. You will receive information about the outcome via e-mail to your u:account e-mail address.

reranked.text: after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: / - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text: apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would like to take a leave of absence. Is that possible? Yes, you can also take a leave of absence at the VWU. To do so, submit an application for a leave of absence within the relevant deadline. The above-mentioned reasons for a leave of absence apply. - Does the leave of absence end at the end of the semesters I applied for or is it automatically extended? The leave of absence ends automatically. If you wish to extend your leave of absence, you must submit a new application within the respective deadline and provide the necessary documents. - When can I take examinations again after my leave of absence has ended? From the start of the next semester, you can take examinations again, attend courses, submit academic work for assessment and submit applications for recognition. You may also take examinations from the semester in which you were on a leave after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
reranked.text: Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the relevant authority whether you can receive family or student financial aid. - Insurance: Co-insurance with relatives or student self-insurance may not be possible. Please enquire directly with your insurance company.

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text:
How to apply for a leave of absence:

1. Scan the required documents. Merge them into a PDF file using an online tool of your choice.
2. Complete the online form in the [Service Desk](https://servicedesk.univie.ac.at/plugins/servlet/desk/portal/42/create/742) within the deadline and add the required documents as attachments (at the top of the online form).

After your application has been processed, you will receive an e-mail to your u:account e-mail address. As soon as the Student's Union fee has been received in [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/) > Financial matters > Tuition fee/Student's Union fee, you can print out your study sheet under Personal matters > My documents.

Depending on the semester and the reason for your application, you must meet different deadlines:

reranked.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: Leave of absence during the semester
reranked.text: A leave of absence during the semester is only possible if you can prove that one of the following reasons occurred and only became relevant after the start of the semester: / - Pregnancy Proof: - parent-child pass with calculated date of birth or medical confirmation and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. - Childcare Proof: - confirmation of registration ("Meldebestätigung") of the applicant and - confirmation of registration ("Meldebestätigung") of the child and - birth certificate of the child and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. Please note: - If you raise your own children, a leave of absence is possible until the children reach the age of legal majority. Both parents can be on leave at the same time. - Care for relatives Proof: - proof of kinship (birth/marriage certificate) and - notification of care level or medical confirmation regarding the need for care (no diagnosis required) and - statutory declaration from the applicant stating that the relative is being cared for by the applicant and - proof or statutory declaration of the extent to which it is an unforeseeable and inevitable event. - Medical condition that verifiably prevents progress in the degree programme Proof: - [medical confirmation (PDF)](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Beurlaubung/Formular_medizinische_Bestaetigung_Beurlaubung_EN.pdf) confirming that you have been or will

Proof: - summons, official order or other confirmation letter by an authority - Current situation in Ukraine Due to the current situation in Ukraine, students from Ukraine and the Russian Federation can apply for leave of absence in summer semester 2026 until 28 February. Write a short explanation for your prevention from studying in the online form in the Servicedesk.

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: Reimbursement of the tuition fee Already paid tuition fee can be reimbursed for certain, precisley defined reasons. This is the case, for example, if your application for remission or leave of absence has been granted. Further examples: You have completed your studies before the end of the admission period or you have paid too much. Related Links: - [Reimbursement of tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) Related entries: - [Leave of absence](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=30834&cHash=6c0061df445fb72ace014b66819ed159) - [Tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31268&cHash=5186d8ac34fa30c4b1270fc725ce2ef7) - [Remission of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31930&cHash=ec520748e744f3ee49dc41584e03d431) - Remission of the tuition fee Citizens of an EU/EEA country or equivalent non-EU/EEA citizens can apply for remission of the tuition fee provided that: - it has been submitted on time and in full and - there is a recognised reason for remission. The application for remission is granted, but you have already paid the tuition fee? Read the information on tuition fee reimbursement and note the application deadlines. Related Links: - [Remission](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) - [Reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) - [Tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/) Related entries: - [Equal status with EU/EEA citizens with regard to the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31127&cHash=a9fb293a7c3370b27dd98f693d1ca082) - [Reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31959&cHash=7d2042e16b5c6049dc06cc8398971bfa) - Residence permit ("visa") Residence permits authorise persons from third countries to settle in Austria (for a limited period) and/or to work here. Please enquire about this topic at an Austrian representation authority (embassy, consulate) or the Municipal Department 35 (MA 35). A residence permit can also have an influence on the amount of

reranked.title: Remission and reimbursement
heading_path: Reimbursement of the tuition fee/Students' Union fee
reranked.text: the tuition fee is prescribed at any other Austrian University? A reimbursement in this case is only possible if you: - were admitted at the University of Vienna in the previous semester or - have not participated in any examinations and have not submitted any academic papers for grading in the current semester. - Application for leave of absence granted After paying the tuition fee your [application for leave of absence](/en/admission/leave-of-absence/) was granted? A reimbursement is only possible if you have submitted your application for leave of absence before 30 November (for the winter semester) and 30 April (for the summer semester). - Application for remission granted Your application for remission of the tuition fee was granted after paying the tuition fee? The tuition fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too high You have paid a too high fee? The difference to the prescribed fee can be reimbursed. - The fee paid was too little You have paid too little and are therefore no longer admitted at the University of Vienna? The fee that you have paid can be reimbursed. - Reimbursement of the Students' Union fee The Students' Union fee does not remain at the University of Vienna. It is forwarded to the Students' Union. Therefore you have to submit your application for reimbursement [directly at the Students' Union](https://www.oeh.ac.at/en/forms/oh-fee-refund/). The application is

reranked.title: ABC of terminology
reranked.text: of a degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=42309&cHash=54e30ba32dac123141345da256f85101) - Educational institution ID The educational institution ID is a unique label of a higher education institution and precedes the degree programme code. Both can be found in the Study overview on u:space and on the student record sheet. Related Links: - [u:space](https://uspace.univie.ac.at/en/web/gast) Related entries: - [u:space](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31311&cHash=571431f2f0b27f0eec7e3fbf0fcdd580) - Educational leave An educational leave is a time off from work to complete education and continue education and training. During an educational leave, the Austrian Public Employment Service (AMS) pays a continuing education allowance under certain prerequisites. Please obtain information directly from the AMS. As part of educational leave, you can study in a non-degree programme. Please consider the consequences of a non-degree programme if you have a secondary-school leaving certificate. Related Links: - [Non-degree programme](/en/admission/non-degree-programme/) - [Austrian Public Employment Service](https://www.ams.at/arbeitsuchende/topicliste/bildungskarenz-oesterreich) (in German) Related entries: - [Non-degree programme](/en/abc-of-terminology/related-entries/?tx_irfaq_pi1%5BshowUid%5D=31904&cHash=afc9b783c1eacf56ab1f871f6e92ad54) - Eligible degree programme The admission to a master programme is only possible if you have previously completed an eligible degree programme. After having applied for admission to the degree programme, the Admission Office will evaluate the eligibility of your previous degree. If it is found to be eligible, but some exams (of max. 30 ECTS) are missing, supplementray exams are prescribed in your admission letter. The Master Access Guide illustrates which degree programmes enable you to continue with a certain master programme. Related Links: - Master Access Guide Related

reranked.title: Leave of absence
reranked.text:
You have the option of [closing your studies](/en/admission/closing-a-degree-programme/) and resuming them at a later date. Consider this option especially if it is likely that your studies will be interrupted for a long time. Please inform yourself in advance about possible conditions for [resuming your studies](/en/admission/resumption-of-studies/). For master programmes with an admission procedure and the bachelor programme in Psychology, you must successfully complete the entrance exam/selection procedure again. For the bachelor programme in Sport Science and the teaching subject in Sports and Physical Education, you may have to take the [sporting aptitude admission test](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/zulassung-zum-studium/ergaenzungspruefung/) again. Please contact the [SSC Sport Science](https://lehre-schmelz.univie.ac.at/studienservicecenter-und-studienprogrammleitung/) for more information.

As an alternative to a leave of absence, you may apply for a [remission of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/). Please note the respective application deadlines and reasons for remission.

- [§ 21 Study Law of the Statutes of the University of Vienna](https://satzung.univie.ac.at/en/study-law/)
- [Universities Act 2002 (UG), § 67](https://www.ris.bka.gv.at/GeltendeFassung.wxe?Abfrage=Bundesnormen&Gesetzesnummer=20002128)

reranked.title: Remission and reimbursement
reranked.text:
/

- Remission due to illness: What qualifies als illness?

  Reasons for illness may be, among other things, major operations, chronic illness or also mental impairments. You need a [confirmation by a medical specialist](/fileadmin/user_upload/p_studieren/Studienzulassung/Formulare/Formular_Fachaerztliche_Bestaetigung_Erlass_EN.pdf), that you are thereby impaired from your studies for at least two months during the semester. The diagnosis does not need to be stated.
- What shall I do if I do not have all proofs for applying for remission until the application deadline?

  In this case you must pay the tuition fee. Afterwards, you will be able to apply for remission and reimbursement at the same time. These two applications can be submitted for the winter semester until the following 28/29 February and for the summer semester until 30 September.
- My application for remission of the tuition fee was granted, but I have already paid the tuition fee. What can I do?

  If your application for remission was granted, you can apply for the reimbursement of the tuition fee in order to get the money transferred back to your bank account. The application for reimbursement can also be submitted at the same time as the application for remission of the tuition fee.
- What happens after I have applied for remission/reimbursement?

  The processing of your application takes approximately four weeks. You will receive information about the outcome via e-mail to your u:account e-mail address.

selected.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
selected.text: fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

selected.title: Leave of absence
heading_path: What do you need to consider?
selected.text: If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending

heading_path: What do you need to consider?

You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester

selected.title: Leave of absence
selected.text:
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

You may take examinations (including those from the semester in which you took a leave of absence) after the leave of absence has ended. - Effects on the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee - You are exempt from paying the tuition fee during your leave of absence. However, the leave of absence does not exempt you from paying the Student's Union fee. Make sure you pay this fee on time, otherwise your studies will be automatically closed. - You have already paid the tuition fee and are now on a leave of absence? A [reimbursement of the tuition fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester

[4] Leave of absence
A leave of absence is only possible for precisely defined reasons. You cannot apply for a leave of absence for an individual programme. It affects all your studies at the University of Vienna.

Please read the consequences of a leave of absence on your studies carefully. Afterwards you can decide whether you want to apply for a leave of absence or not.

Submit the application before the start of the semester within the relevant deadline. During the semester, a leave of absence is only possible for specific reasons. You will also need additional confirmation.

If you are attending a university course, please contact the [ServiceCenter of the Postgraduate Center](https://postgraduatecenter.at/en/about-us/contact/). Doctoral/PhD students should contact [doktorat.beurlaubung@univie.ac.at](javascript:linkTo_UnCryptMailto('ocknvq,fqmvqtcv0dgwtncwdwpiBwpkxkg0ce0cv');).

- Winter semester 2026/27: from 13 July to 30 September 2026
- Summer semester 2027: from 11 January to 28 February 2027

- Summer semester 2026: from 1 March to 30 September 2026
- Winter semester 2026/27: from 1 October 2026 to 28 February 2027
- Summer semester 2027: from 1 March to 30 September 2027

The leave of absence exempts you from the tuition fee, but not from the Student's Union fee. Please pay this fee in due time. Otherwise, your studies at the University of Vienna will be automatically closed.

[5] Leave of absence — FAQ
after the leave of absence has ended. - What information must be included on the confirmation letter from my place of employment? Your place of employment must confirm that your employment exceeds the marginal earnings threshold ("Geringfügikeitsgrenze") and lasts more than four weeks in the semester you applied for. In the case of training/qualification programmes, you also need a confirmation of participation/registration from the training/qualification institute stating the extent and duration of the course. - What documentation can I provide to prove that I am self-employed? You can provide a corresponding confirmation (such as a tax book extract, health insurance contract etc.) in combination with a statutory declaration on the extent of the impairment. - I applied for a leave of absence shortly before the payment deadline for the tuition fee/ Students' Union fee. Should I still pay the fee? Yes, otherwise your studies will be closed automatically. If your application is approved, you can apply for a [reimbursement](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) of the tuition fee. You must pay the Students' Union fee in any case. It cannot be refunded.

[6] Leave of absence — FAQ
/ - Is there a limit to how often I can apply for a leave of absence? There is no limit to how often you can apply for a leave of absence. Please note that you will have to resubmit the application once your leave of absence has ended and provide the required documents again. - Do semesters on leave of absence count towards the minimum number of credits? No, semesters on leave of absence are not included in the semester count. Please Note: Only leaves of absence taken during the first four semesters for which you have enrolled will be taken into account. Example: You have to achieve your minimum number of credits by the summer semester of 2026. The deadline for this is 31 October 2026. If you take a leave of absence in the winter semester of 2026, it will be too late. You will lose your admission to the program if you do not achieve the minimum number of credits by the deadline. - Can I take a leave of absence from a semester that is already over? No, that is not possible. You can only apply before the start of the semester within the respective deadlines for one or two semesters. After the start of the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. - I am attending the VWU and would

prompt_messages.content:
You are a concise assistant for prospective and current University of Vienna students. Answer the question using ONLY the numbered sources provided.
Rules:
- Base every statement on the sources; never add outside knowledge or guess.
- Cite each claim inline with the bracket number(s) of the source(s) you used, e.g. [1] or [2][3]. Cite only sources you actually relied on.
- If the sources do not contain the answer, reply that you don't know, and do NOT cite any sources.
- Keep the answer short and direct (it may be read aloud by a voice assistant).

Sources:
[1] Leave of absence — What do you need to consider?
fee](/en/tuition-fee/remission-and-reimbursement/) is only possible if you apply for a leave of absence in the first half of the semester by a certain deadline. For a leave of absence in the winter semester this is 30 November, for the summer semester it is 30 April. - If the application for a leave of absence is not granted, you must pay the tuition fee/Students' Union fee as prescribed. The same applies if you withdraw an approved application. - Effects on other parties - Studying at another university: If you wish to take a leave of absence at another university, you must also submit an application there. - Residence permit: The approval of a leave of absence does not automatically lead to the issue or extension of a residence permit. Clarify the possible consequences of the leave of absence in terms of immigration and residence law with the relevant authority before submitting your application. If you live in Vienna, [Municipal Department 35](https://www.wien.gv.at/english/administration/immigration/) is responsible for your residence permit. If you live in another province, your [district administration](https://www.oesterreich.gv.at/orgsearch/) is the competent authority. There you can ask about all aspects of this topic. - University Preparation Programme of the Vienna Universities (VWU): A leave of absence during the VWU is possible. Make sure you clarify the effects on your residence permit with the relevant authority before submitting your application! - Financial aid: Clarify with the

[2] Leave of absence — What do you need to consider?
If you apply for a leave of absence, this may have the following consequences: / - Effects on your studies - The admission to the degree programme remains valid. You can continue to use the u:account services and the library. - The semester count is suspended (including the [minimum number of credits](/en/minimum-number-of-credits/)). - You will only receive the student record sheet and no confirmation of enrolment. - The u:card cannot be validated as a student ID for the semester on leave. - A leave of absence does not stop an automatic transfer to a new curriculum. - A leave of absence before the start of the semester can be granted for one or two semesters. In the case of a leave of absence during the semester, the application can only be approved for the current semester. The leave of absence ends automatically. Submit a new application for the following semester within the application deadline to continue your leave of absence. - Depending on the reason for leave of absence and the documents submitted, the application will be approved for one or two semesters. If the reason for your leave of absence continues to exist after this (e.g. due to caring for your own children), you can submit a new application. - Effects on examinations and courses Taking examinations, submitting an application for recognition, submitting academic work for assessment and attending
